title: The Feast of Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: May 28, 2023

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

May 28, 2023 was Whit Sunday (Pentecost), the seventh Sunday of Easter and one of the three principal feasts of the Christian year. Under the Revised Common Lectionary, the readings would have been Acts 2:1-21 (the descent of the Holy Spirit), Psalm 104:25-35, 37b, 1 Corinthians 12:3b-13, and John 7:37-39, focusing on the gift of the Spirit and the birth of the Church. This falls within the Easter Season, concluding the 50 days of resurrection celebration that began on Easter Sunday (April 9, 2023).

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the lead-up to its General Convention, scheduled for July 2023 in Louisville, Kentucky, which would address significant internal divisions over sexuality and inclusion. In May 2023, various dioceses including Colorado were preparing resolutions and delegate slates for what was expected to be a contentious gathering. The Diocese of Colorado under Bishop Kym Lucas was actively engaged in these preparations, though no major local Episcopal news dominated the immediate week before this sermon.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical late May weather—warm and dry with afternoon thunderstorm potential. The Colorado legislature had concluded its 2023 session on May 10, leaving local political attention on implementation of new laws. Saint John's Cathedral sits in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, which was experiencing ongoing development and gentrification discussions. No major crises or breaking local news dominated the specific week of May 22-28, 2023.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The primary national story was the ongoing debt ceiling negotiations, with Congress voting to pass the Fiscal Responsibility Act on May 23, 2023, averting a default crisis. Former President Donald Trump faced multiple legal proceedings, with his classified documents trial underway in Florida. The Supreme Court had recently ruled on affirmative action cases (decided June 29, but oral arguments and discussion were active in late May). The general political atmosphere remained highly polarized heading into the 2024 campaign cycle.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The war in Ukraine remained active with Russia conducting ongoing operations; Kyiv experienced air raids in late May. Turkey's recent presidential election (May 28, 2023 runoff) was occurring on the exact date of this sermon, with Recep Tayyip Erdogan securing reelection. China-Taiwan tensions remained elevated with ongoing military posturing and diplomatic friction.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in late May 2023 was characterized by post-pandemic social reopening and summer entertainment season beginning. The 2023 NBA Finals were ongoing (Denver Nuggets had won the championship on June 12, so anticipation was high in the region). AI and ChatGPT dominated cultural conversation following the technology's viral adoption in late 2022. There was a broader sense of economic uncertainty with banking failures earlier in spring (SVB, etc.) still affecting public confidence, alongside inflation concerns and ongoing labor movement activity.
